meaning: adj.非被强迫的,自然的,不勉强的,不费力的
examples: Learn the unforced rhythms of grace.
China should be a constant topic of unforced conversation.
Federer wound up with 66 unforced errors, 28 more than Djokovic.
Jankovic won with steady play from the baseline, while the big-swinging Muguruza committed 29 unforced errors.
It has an easy, unforced rhythm, yet it needs more plot, more complication, more conflict.
